steps.title: Find the small height
steps.content:
Triangle $ABC$ is right, with hypotenuse $AB=\sqrt{34}$ and leg $AC=3$. Use the Pythagorean theorem to find $BC$.
$$
BC^2+3^2=(\sqrt{34})^2
$$
$$
BC^2=34-9=25
$$
$$
BC=5
$$

steps.title: Scale to the larger triangle
steps.content:
Since $AC=3$ and $CE=21$, the larger base is
$$
AE=3+21=24
$$
The right triangles are similar, so the scale factor from $AC$ to $AE$ is
$$
\frac{24}{3}=8
$$
Thus the corresponding height is
$$
DE=5(8)=40
$$

steps.title: Compute the area
steps.content: Use $AE$ as the base and $DE$ as the height. $$ \text{Area}=\frac{1}{2}(24)(40)=480 $$ The answer is 480.
